name: A Basque getaway starting from Castets
name_original: Une escapade basque à partir de Castets
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Urt are situated in a region that offers varied terrain for cyclists. The area features routes that follow rivers, such as the Adour, and extend towards coastal areas. Cyclists can expect a mix of flat sections and gentle ascents, providing diverse experiences. The landscape primarily consists of open countryside and riverine environments, suitable for extended rides. Best touring cycling routes around Urt The most popular touring cycling route is La Marquèze Bridge – Saubusse loop from Urt, a 36.2 miles (58.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the local river landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of the Adour River – Cannon at Pont de Laxague loop from Urt, an easy 15.1 miles (24.3 km) path. This route follows the Adour River, providing a scenic and relatively flat ride. Local touring cyclists also love the Marengo Bridge – Beach road loop from Urt, a 46.5 miles (74.8 km) trail leading through open countryside and towards coastal roads, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Urt is defined by river paths, open countryside, and routes extending to coastal areas. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance rides. The routes in Urt are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 390 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Urt's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Urt?
A: There are nearly 30 touring cycling routes around Urt, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Urt?
A: Yes, Urt offers several easy touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20521780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Adour River – Cannon at Pont de Laxague loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 15.1-mile path that follows the Adour River, providing a scenic and relatively flat ride, ideal for a relaxed outing.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Urt?
A: The touring cycling routes around Urt feature varied terrain, including flat sections along rivers like the Adour and gentle ascents. You'll primarily encounter open countryside and riverine environments, with some routes extending towards coastal areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Urt?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Urt are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41665515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marquèze Bridge – Saubusse loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt; is a 36.2-mile circular trail offering views of the local river landscape.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see along the cycling routes near Urt?
A: While cycling around Urt, you can explore several interesting spots. Highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5248143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bastide of Hastingues&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5091791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gramont Castle in Bidache&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6036001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hastingues Village&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that lead to coastal areas from Urt?
A: Yes, some routes from Urt extend towards the coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41668055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marengo Bridge – Beach road loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt; is a 46.5-mile trail that takes you through open countryside and along coastal roads. For a longer coastal experience, consider the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41667155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Little Bayonne – Grande Plage of Biarritz loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Urt?
A: The touring cycling routes in Urt are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, scenic river paths, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there routes suitable for advanced touring cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Yes, for more experienced touring cyclists, there are challenging routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41667155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Little Bayonne – Grande Plage of Biarritz loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 68-mile route with significant elevation gain, offering a substantial ride towards coastal destinations.

Q: Can I find routes that avoid main roads for a more peaceful cycling experience?
A: Yes, the area around Urt includes dedicated cycling paths designed to offer a more tranquil experience away from heavy traffic. One such highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2666167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cycle path to avoid the main road&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into your touring plans.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes around Urt?
A: Route durations vary depending on distance and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20521780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Adour River – Cannon at Pont de Laxague loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41665515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marquèze Bridge – Saubusse loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt;, typically take over 3 hours, while more difficult and longer routes can extend to 6 hours or more.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Basque region starting from Urt or nearby?
A: While Urt itself is not explicitly described as a Basque region hub in the provided data, a route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e862583429/a-basque-getaway-starting-from-castets" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Basque getaway starting from Castets&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 23.3-mile touring cycling experience that delves into the broader Basque area, providing a taste of the regional landscape.
tips: Guiche Port is located along the towpath.

View from the Came-Guiche cycle path along the Bidouze. The ruins of the castle are located near the village of Bidache. Various shops on site. If you decide to refuel, you will have to leave the track and go up towards the village. You can join the track at the exit of Bidache, direction Guiche Port.
